about:
Erika A. Pabo MD MBA is a visionary executive leader and entrepreneur with deep experience building operating and transforming businesses across the value-based healthcare ecosystem. This experience ranges across the business development life cycle – from building zero to one to transforming enterprise scaled businesses – all focused on delivering quadruple aim outcomes. 

Dr. Pabo currently serves as Chief Transformation Officer & Operations Leader for Humana’s Primary Care Organization (PCO) and SVP Humana. The PCO is a $4.3B revenue care delivery business that operates as Centerwell and Conviva. In this role she wears two primary hats: 1) Operations: responsible for the core operations of the business including medical risk adjustment quality revenue cycle management patient service center transportation credentialing training associate experience and government affairs and 2) Transformation: trend management process improvement customer experience and innovation to ensure that the business continues to improve its operating leverage as it grows rapidly as well as innovate as consumer needs evolve. 

Dr. Pabo previously served Co-President for the Author business segment which was a dual transformation company incubated and operated as a business segment within Humana. In her Co-President role she led business and growth strategy and had P&L responsibility for the business. She also continued to serve as Chief Health Officer for Author where she designed built and operated a transformed experience for seniors enabling deeper more trusting customer relationships supporting their whole health needs and using new tools and technology to eliminate both barriers to care as well as back-office inefficiencies. Parts of this business have now been integrated into the larger company to serve as core for modernization of Humana’s Medicare and Medicaid businesses and other parts have been spun out into new joint venture.

She continues to serve as Faculty at Harvard Medical School and Associate Faculty at Ariadne Labs and to practice medicine at Brigham and Women’s Hospital Advanced Primary Care South Huntington. She is a sought-after speaker talking regularly at national healthcare conferences on wide ranging topics in value-based care and business transformation.

Dr. Pabo earned a BA with honors from Yale College an MD from Harvard Medical School and an MBA from Harvard Business School (Robert S. Kaplan Fellow). She completed her residency at Brigham and Women’s Hospital and is Board Certified by the ABIM.

about:
In 1986 Tom founded Farallon Capital Management a San Francisco-based hedge fund that pioneered the multi-strategy approach to investing. At the time of its founding as a risk arbitrage firm Farallon had less than $10M in assets. Under Tom's leadership the firm expanded into multiple new strategies and geographies and grew to $36B in assets. During this time Tom also served as a partner and member of the Executive Committee at Hellman & Friedman a San Francisco–based private equity firm. In 2012 Tom left both roles in order to give his time money and energy to fight for climate issues.

Before California firmly established itself as a leader in climate policy Tom worked to help make it the largest jurisdiction in the world with a 100% clean energy law and prevented the oil and gas industry's effort to roll back the state's climate protections. Around that time he also cofounded Beneficial State Bank a triple bottom line community development bank dedicated to economic justice and environmental sustainability.

In 2013 he founded NextGen America the largest youth voter engagement organization in American history which has registered over 1.5 million young voters over the last 10 years. NextGen is credited with not only helping to drive record numbers of young voters to the polls in the 2020 US presidential election but also for its influence in making climate a significant issue on ballots.

Tom is also the founder of NextGen Policy a California-based policy organization focused on climate environmental social and economic legislative advocacy and civic engagement. In 2022 the organization helped navigate 26 bills through the legislative process that were signed into law. Over the last two years the organization has helped to secure nearly $60 billion in the state budget to support organizational priorities.

In 2020 he ran for president to talk about climate change and other issues he thought were being overlooked and went on to serve as California Governor Gavin Newsom's top economic advisor on the Business and Jobs Recovery Task Force.

Tom is currently running for governor of California to tackle the affordability crisis take on corporate interests and deliver for Californians.
current_position: Democratic Candidate for Governor of California

about:
I didn't go to IIT. I worked call centres at 18.

In 2002 I juggled college in the morning and a call centre seat in the evening to support my family. My mother a small-town Punjab girl turned first-generation entrepreneur taught me the only thing that's ever mattered: when one door shuts you build your own house.
That line has run in my head for over two decades.
It carried me through One97 where I grew through the ranks and the company sponsored my MBA. Then Aircel where I built content at a scale most founders never touch. Then into entrepreneurship because the top 4 FAANG didn't return my calls. A non-IIT non-IIM kid doesn't get the interview no matter what he can actually do.
So I built. Carbun8. Unpuzzleit. Quiet bootstrapped years. Year by year I bought back everything my family lost in the 1992 crash. Changing family karma one repayment at a time.
Then I saw the gap that became Vama.
India is the world's most spiritual civilisation and almost none of it is online. Lakhs of temples a billion devotees at home tens of millions more in the diaspora and the digital infrastructure for the most important part of their lives is a patchwork of WhatsApp forwards and middlemen. The category was hiding in plain sight because the people building Indian internet weren't praying at the temples we grew up in.
Today Vama.app is India's spiritual super-app. Puja. Astrology. Spiritual commerce. Vama TV vernacular devotional microdramas for the audience Bollywood forgot. Backed by Wavemaker 500 Global and Silver Needle.
Here's the part most founders don't write.
Spirituality came to me late. When things went wrong I blamed everyone including god. Slowly I understood he wasn't punishing me. He was clearing the path. Today I'm more connected to Shiva than I've ever been. Vama started because I saw a market gap. It continues because somewhere along the way it stopped being just a market.
I'm building this for the devotee in Manchester who hasn't done a puja for his father in years. For the grandmother in Lucknow who wants to send prasad to her grandkids in Toronto. For the GenZ kid in Bengaluru more spiritual than the boomers think. For the small temple in the hills whose pujari has never been to a city.
And for the 18-year-old in the call centre who didn't know the doors that wouldn't open for him were the wrong doors.
If you're building in FaithTech Indian internet or the diaspora I'd love to talk. If you're a temple an astrologer or a devotee with something to tell us we are listening.
We're just getting started.

company_description:
Gradalis is a late-stage clinical biotechnology company developing immunotherapies for multiple cancer indications including an upcoming Phase 3 registration trial in patients with ovarian cancer. The company's proprietary bi-shRNA therapy platform which has been proven in its clinical studies can be utilized to silence a broad range of genes and is applicable to multiple cancer types. 

About Vigil
Vigil® is the first cellular therapy to show positive survival benefits in a randomized controlled clinical study of patients with solid tumors. Vigil is a self-navigating cellular immunotherapy that addresses the major deficits of leading cancer treatments. Vigil uses all of a patient's tumor-related antigens to identify cancer cells and then enhances the training of the immune system to recognize and attack those cells. Gradalis's proprietary bi-shRNA platform accomplishes this via three key functions: (1) it blocks furin the enzyme that generates the immunosuppressive cytokines TGF beta types 1 and 2 (2) it activates effector cells including antigen-presenting dendritic cells and CD8+ T cells via the production of GM-CSF and (3) it induces an immune response to the full complement of neo-antigens from the patient's tumor via intradermal administration.

In a Phase 2b trial Vigil showed a positive trend in the primary endpoint of recurrence-free survival in the overall population and a significant improvement in recurrence-free survival and overall survival in a pre-planned subgroup analysis of Stage III/IV newly diagnosed ovarian cancer patients with the BRCA wild type (BRCA-wt) molecular profile. The company is preparing to initiate a clinical study intended for product registration in patients in this subgroup. Additionally Phase 1 results in an all-comer trial have shown positive signals of activity in multiple tumor types.

company_description:
World Labs is a spatial intelligence company building Large World Models (LWMs) to perceive generate and interact with the 3D world. We aim to lift AI models from the 2D plane of pixels to full 3D worlds – both virtual and real – endowing them with spatial intelligence as rich as our own. 

We believe that humans are innovative curious and creative science and technology are both manifestations and drivers of these impulses. Propelling AI forward with spatial intelligence will also propel forward both individuals and humanity as a whole. Toward this goal World Labs will develop spatially intelligent LWMs that can understand and reason about the 3D world from images and other modalities. Over time we expect to train increasingly powerful models with broader capabilities that can be applied in a variety of domains working alongside people.

These are ambitious goals so we are building an ambitious company to tackle them. Our team led by co-founders Fei-Fei Li PhD Justin Johnson PhD Christoph Lassner PhD and Ben Mildenhall PhD has a strong research background but we are not motivated by exploration for its own sake. Instead we believe that now is a unique moment where rapid scientific progress has thinned the barrier between research and applications. We aim to seize this opportunity focusing on the entire throughline from research to engineering to product to people. 

We are bringing together the most formidable slate of pixel talent ever assembled creating a tight feedback loop between our spatially intelligent foundation models and products that will empower our users. We invite you to learn more about joining our fast-growing team.
headline: Cofounder/CEO World Labs AI Researcher & Professor Stanford University Co-Director Stanford Human-Centered AI Institute Au

Claire is the author of 9 books that have been translated into 12 languages including One Minute Mentoring (with her mentor legendary management guru Ken Blanchard) Social Media Success for Every Brand and Twitter for Good. Her next book explores executive coaching. 

In Claire’s work she has been called everything from “The Woman Who Got the Pope on Twitter”(Wired) & “Twitter’s Pontiff Recruitment Chief” (The Washington Post) to a “Force for Good” (Forbes) & one of the “Ten Most Generous in Social Media” (Fast Company). In a quote not soon to be forgotten (LOL) TechCrunch recently called Claire "a globe-trotting multi-hyphenate polymath." Claire was one of the first users of Twitter in Kenya (a claim verified by no one) and is also known for the dubious honor of being the first person to live-tweet her own child’s birth which was admittedly a bad idea. 

Prior to joining 100 Coaches the premier leadership firm focused on executive coaching Claire was a venture capitalist and (always still) marketer. She was also an advisor to Antler a global early stage accelerator with $1.2BAUM across 23 global locations and startup chair at a decentralized web3 fund of 150 professional venture capitalists from the Kauffman Fellows program where she was a venture capital fellow. Claire holds an MBA from Oxford University where she was a Skoll Foundation Scholar for Social Entrepreneurship and has a BA in History and an MA in Anthropology from Stanford University. She was the co-founder of Hope Runs a non-profit organization operating in Kenya from 2006 to 2019.

current_position_description: Acquired by Elevance Health in December 2024. CareBridge exists to improve the lives of people receiving long-term care services in their homes providing longitudinal medical care through an interdisciplinary care team 24/7 as well as Decision Support around home and community based services (HCBS) utilization. CareBridge's solutions offer efficiencies for states health plans and providers and security and peace of mind for people receiving services and their families. These offerings have proven to keep individuals healthy and safe in their own home while promoting independence and maximizing their quality of life.
company_description:
CareBridge is the nation's leading value-based care provider for members receiving home and community-based services (HCBS). CareBrige partners with managed Medicaid and Medicare plans to maximize the health independence and quality of life for members receiving HCBS. CareBridge's solutions include 24/7 Member Support Decision Support Electronic Visit Verification (EVV) and Data Aggregation.
 
24/7 Member Support is CareBridge's clinical model designed to improve health outcomes for individuals receiving home and community-based services. CareBridge's virtual-first program provides members and their caregivers with a cellular-enabled tablet that gives them access 24 hours a day 7 days a week to CareBridge's interdisciplinary medical team of physicians psychiatrists pharmacists nurses and social workers.
 
Decision Support is an innovative solution that provides actionable recommendations on the type of home and community-based services that will help members achieve optimal independence in their lives. To develop these recommendations CareBridge's LTSS Specialists each of whom have a background in physical or occupational therapy complete a person-centered assessment of each individual informed by insights from the nation's largest HCBS analytical database to identify potential enhancements to the individual's care plan and recommend empowering alternatives that can keep the member safe and independent at home.
 
CareBridge's solutions have been shown to increase independence improve clinical quality and reduce emergency room visits hospitalizations and total costs all while receiving a Net Promoter Score of 84 from patients and families.
headline: CFO at CareBridge Health

company_description: HAYA Therapeutics is a precision medicines company developing RNA-guided programmable therapeutics targeting the regulatory genome for serious health conditions including cardiovascular disease and cancer. The company is using its innovative cell-state modifying platform to gain novel insights into the biology of long non-coding RNAs (lncRNAs) the 'dark genomes' information processing features and 'source code'. HAYA's lead therapeutic candidate is HTX-001 an antisense oligonucleotide targeting Wisper which is a tissue and cell-specific cardiac lncRNA known to play a role in heart failure. The company is also developing a pipeline of lncRNA-targeting candidates for the cell-specific treatment of diseases in other tissues including the lungs and the microenvironment of solid tumor cancers. Headquartered at the life sciences park Biopôle in Lausanne Switzerland with laboratory facilities at Lilly Gateway Labs powered by Alexandria in San Diego HAYA is led by a world-class team of experts in programmable and precision RNA-targeted therapeutics and is supported by a strong investor consortium. HAYA's mission is to identify and develop breakthrough disease-modifying therapeutics with the potential for greater efficacy safety and accessibility than existing treatments.

about:
I spent 6 years in VC evaluating startups and helping founders grow. Along the way I saw how messy and time-consuming investment research can be - digging through reports pulling data from different sources and trying to piece everything together.

Three years ago I switched sides and started building startups myself. That experience only reinforced what I already knew: research slows people down whether you're an investor operator or founder.

That's why we're building Hercule - starting with VC and M&A - to help professionals cut through the noise and get to the insights that actually matter faster.

Since launching we've seen early traction:
→ Teams signing up to streamline their workflows
→ Trial users saying they can't imagine going back to manual research
→ Investors and operators validating the problem we're solving

And we're just getting started.

My focus in on making Hercule into something indispensable an AI-powered tool that saves professionals hours every day while surfacing insights they might have missed.

If you're in M&A or VC and deal with research inefficiencies let's chat.

about:
The next decade of the creator economy will be defined by ownership — not just of content but of the entire business model behind it: revenue community data and distribution.

As CEO of Uscreen my focus is simple: help creators build durable scalable subscription businesses with full control over how they monetize and engage with their audience. We give them one place to run it all — video catalogs live streaming and OTT memberships communities analytics and distribution across web mobile and TV — so they can grow with clarity flexibility and confidence.

Uscreen powers this shift at scale. More than 13 000 creators and media companies have used our platform to build and grow their video businesses generating nearly $1 billion in GMV reaching 15 million end users and streaming over 8 billion minutes of content worldwide. From fitness and education to coaching sports and media & entertainment creators use Uscreen to launch memberships build communities and—when they're ready—expand into their own branded video apps.

My career has centered around helping entrepreneurs build stable scalable businesses in industries where predictability is anything but guaranteed. That's why Uscreen is such a natural fit. We give creators the infrastructure to build recurring revenue and long-term resilience not just short-term visibility.

At Uscreen I'm focused on three things:
1) building the leading platform for creator-owned video businesses — flexible enough for creators at every stage 
2) scaling a world-class team aligned around that vision and
3) giving creators the clarity tools and control to build predictable sustainable businesses on their own terms.

The creator economy is maturing and the next wave belongs to those who build ecosystems — not just audiences — and own the entire experience.

If you're building in video memberships fitness education or the future of digital media I'd love to connect. We're just getting started.
current_position_description:
Uscreen is an all-in-one video monetization platform that empowers creators and media companies to build and scale membership-based video businesses. To date more than 13 000 creators have used Uscreen to generate over $1B in GMV serving 15M+ end users and streaming over 8B minutes of content across fitness education coaching sports and media & entertainment.

What I focus on:

Scaling Uscreen's long-term vision and positioning the company as the leading platform for creator-owned video businesses

Driving predictable growth through clear GTM strategy strong financial operations and subscription revenue expansion

Building and developing a world-class leadership team across product marketing sales and customer experience

Ensuring creators have the tools to build real businesses — from memberships and community to distribution across web mobile and TV

Championing ownership for creators giving them the clarity and infrastructure to build durable recurring-revenue businesses

company_description: The Wake Forest Institute for Regenerative Medicine is recognized as an international leader in translating scientific discovery into clinical therapies with many world firsts including the development and implantation of the first engineered organ in a patient. Over 450 people at the institute the largest in the world work on more than 40 different tissues and organs. A number of the basic principles of tissue engineering and regenerative medicine were first developed at the institute. WFIRM researchers have successfully engineered replacement tissues and organs in all four categories – flat structures tubular tissues hollow organs and solid organs – and 15 different applications of cell/tissue therapy technologies such as skin urethras cartilage bladders muscle kidney and vaginal organs have been successfully used in human patients. The institute which is part of Wake Forest School of Medicine is located in the Innovation Quarter in downtown Winston-Salem NC and is driven by the urgent needs of patients. The institute is making a global difference in regenerative medicine through collaborations with over 400 entities and institutions worldwide through its government academic and industry partnerships its start-up entities and through major initiatives in breakthrough technologies such as tissue engineering cell therapies diagnostics drug discovery biomanufacturing nanotechnology gene editing and 3D printing.

Prof. Dr. Bruno Holthof is Visiting Professor of Health Innovation in the Nuffield Department of Medicine of Oxford University and Investment Partner of the EQT Health Economics Fund. 

Between October 2015 and June 2022 Bruno was the Chief Executive Officer of Oxford University Hospitals (OUH). OUH employs 12 000 staff across four hospital sites and 44 other locations. During his tenure OUH made significant investments in people and the digital and physical infrastructure supporting the world class research teaching and clinical care taking place in Oxford.

Before OUH he was CEO of the Antwerp Hospital Network from January 2004 until September 2015. During this period he transformed ZNA into the most profitable hospital group in Belgium. Bruno Holthof is also Chair of the Board of Tristel a global infection control and disinfection product company and member of the Board of Financière de Tubize. Tubize is the reference shareholder of UCB a global biopharmaceutical company focused on improving lives of people with severe diseases in immunology and neurology.

Before becoming a CEO he was a partner at McKinsey & Company. During this period he served a wide range of healthcare clients in Europe and the United States and gained significant expertise in the areas of strategy organization and operations. He holds an MBA from the Harvard Business School and an MD/PhD from the University of Leuven.

company_description:
R1 Therapeutics is developing category-defining first-in-class therapies for patients with kidney disease starting with lead asset AP306 for the treatment of hyperphosphatemia in patients with chronic kidney disease (CKD).

R1 launched in March 2026 with an oversubscribed $77.5 million Series A financing and a partnership with Alebund Pharmaceuticals Ltd. to gain exclusive global development and commercial rights outside of Greater China for AP306.

AP306 is a first-in-class pan phosphate active transport inhibitor in development as a monotherapy for the treatment of hyperphosphatemia in patients with CKD. It is distinguished as being the only agent that blocks the "active" transport of phosphate for the treatment of CKD. 

AP306 has been evaluated in a Phase 2a study in dialysis patients demonstrating significant reduction in serum phosphate levels with good safety and tolerability. R1 plans to initiate a Phase 2b study in 2026 to advance the clinical development of AP306 which has the potential to bring a fundamentally new and more effective approach to the management of hyperphosphatemia. 

Headquartered in the San Francisco Bay Area California R1 is backed by a global investor syndicate including Abingworth F-Prime DaVita Venture Group part of DaVita Inc. (NYSE: DVA) Curie.Bio SymBiosis and U.S. Renal Care.

To find out more visit our website: https://r1therapeutics.com/

about:
My focus is investments and financing of innovation-led biotechnology companies. I have three decades in transatlantic venture capital investment banking and private equity experience in life sciences and other sectors. I am currently Senior Partner and member of the Leadership Team of Venture Investments at Novo Holdings focusing on life science investments helping to head this team's European business and operations. Altogether Novo Holdings manages approximately $15 billion in life science investments.

Novo Holdings is a company owned by the NovoNordisk Foundation. It is the holding and investment company of the Novo Group comprising Novo Nordisk and Novonesis and is responsible for managing the Foundation's assets. Novo Holdings is recognized as a one of the world's largest life science investors with a focus on creating long- term value. Novo Holdings provides seed and venture capital to development- stage companies and takes significant ownership positions in growth and well-established companies.

Since the late 90s I have experienced the high and lows of several cycles of biotech investments being involved in over $3bn of life science private/public equity financings in Europe US and Japan. My Board experience covers Boards in US and Europe including Avalyn Pharma (US NASDAQ IPO) Sanifit (Spain M&A CSL) Paratek Pharmaceticals (US NASDAQ IPO) Amolyt Pharma (France M&A AstraZeneca) VectivBio (Switzerland NASDAQ IPO/ M&A Ironwood) Reviral (UK M&A Pfizer) Alentis (Switzerland) F2G (UK) Asceneuron (Switzerland) Windward Bio (Switzerland) and as Board Observer on Numab AG (Switzerland M&A of sub J&J). I represent NH on the LP Adcom of Sofinnova Capital (France) Biogeneration Ventures (Netherlands) Pureos (Switzerland) funds and also on the Novo Holdings Asia's Investment Committee as well as Chair the European Life Sciences Coalition Operating Committee.

Prior to NH I was Partner at Paris based Andera Partners where I co-led a pivotal phase 2 AIM private placement in Verona pharma (UK NASDAQ IPO & M&A Merck).

I initiated my career in VC and investment banking with Nomura in 1998. I have also worked for EFG Corporate Finance KPMG and the UK's NHS. I qualified in Medicine from Guy's & St Thomas's Hospital Medical School London and Accountancy from the ICAEW. I hold a certificate in Company Direction from the UK Institute of Directors. Former Advisor to Ophthalmology Futures Forum and Guest lecturer at University College London Medical School and Paris Descartes University. Regular Chair/Panelist at Life Science Conferences.

company_description:
Alloy Therapeutics is a biotechnology ecosystem company empowering the global scientific community to make better medicines together. From licensing our platforms to working with us as a trusted discovery service partner we have collaborated with over 170+ partners and counting including 18 of Fortune 50 Biopharmaceutical companies to enable the discovery of their next breakthrough biologic medicines. 

With Alloy you can:
• Access our ever-growing stable of antibody and bispecific discovery platforms that blend in vivo and in vitro wet-lab capabilities with AI/ML. Discover with us through our services or by licensing our technologies for use in your lab.
• Unlock intracellular targets with the therapeutic potential of T cell receptor (TCR) modalities through our fully integrated Keyway™ TCRm discovery service offering.
• Reach genetic disease targets at the nucleic acid level with our novel AntiClastic™ ASO Format which enhances the molecule's potency while minimizing off-target interaction and inflammatory response.
• Co-build new biotechnology companies that leverage our core platforms and service capabilities with Alloy's affiliated Venture Studio 82VS. 

Founded in 2017 and privately funded by visionary investors Alloy is headquartered in Boston MA with labs in Cambridge U.K. Basel CH and Athens GA. We reinvest 100% of our revenue back into innovation and access to innovation.

Reach out follow and visit our website to learn more about how we can make better medicines together.

https://www.alloytx.com/

about:
David Topper was the CFO of Arcutis Biotherapeutics a commercial stage biotech focused on developing treatments for unmet needs in immune mediated diseases. Prior to that he was CFO of Inmagene Biopharmaceuticals a clinical stage biotech. He has extensive capital markets experience. He was formerly a Partner for Capital Market at Frazier Healthcare Partners a life sciences focused venture firm investing in both the private and public markets. He was also the CFO and Board Director of Frazier Lifesciences Acquisition Corp. a SPAC sponsored by Frazier Healthcare Partners ( FLAC) which completed a very successful merger. Prior to that he was a Partner at General Atlantic LLC a global growth equity investment firm for 8 years. At GA David managed the firm's relationships with banks coordinated its capital markets activities and provided strategic advice to the firms' portfolio companies. David was a voting member of the firm's Portfolio Committee. During his tenure at GA David served on the boards of multiple portfolio companies including Affinion Meteo TASC Engility and Amherst Pierpont Securities. as well as the boards of TermGrid and CircleUp. He currently serves on the board of Glassbox a public company based in Israel.
 Prior to that David worked in the investment banking industry for 28 years. Most recently he was at J.P.Morgan where he joined in 2005 as Head of Equity Capital Markets. David helped oversee the firm's rise from #6 in market share to #1. He was also Chairman of the Commitments Committee. David led many of the firms major advisory and capital raising assignments in the financial institutions healthcare media auto and other sectors. During his time at J.P.Morgan David also had significant interaction with the U.S. Department of Treasury in connection with a variety of subjects including Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac General Motors and Chrysler Ally Financial and TARP.
 Prior to joining J.P.Morgan David spent 22 years at Morgan Stanley where he served as Head of Equity Capital Markets Managing Director and Chairman of the Commitments Committee. 
 David serves on the Board of the Stanford Institute of Economic Policy Research and previously served on the board of visitors of the Duke University Trinity School of Arts and Sciences the Board of Trustees of the Jewish Museum of New York and The Board of Directors of the Adams Memorial Foundation. He and his wife Peggy live in New York City and have three daughters.

company_description:
Launched in 2006 Amazon Web Services (AWS) began exposing key infrastructure services to businesses in the form of web services -- now widely known as cloud computing. The ultimate benefit of cloud computing and AWS is the ability to leverage a new business model and turn capital infrastructure expenses into variable costs. Businesses no longer need to plan and procure servers and other IT resources weeks or months in advance. Using AWS businesses can take advantage of Amazon's expertise and economies of scale to access resources when their business needs them delivering results faster and at a lower cost.

Today Amazon Web Services provides a highly reliable scalable low-cost infrastructure platform in the cloud that powers hundreds of thousands of businesses in 190 countries around the world. With data center locations in the U.S. Europe Singapore and Japan customers across all industries are taking advantage of our low cost elastic open and flexible secure platform.

about:
Deep Tech Dual Use Companies improving healthcare and security

I am an engineer entrepreneur and venture capitalist and have worked with over 50+ deep tech companies around the world . I started my career as a device physicist with an early stage successful semiconductor company. After spending the 2000s as a VC in the UK I went on to be General Manager the VC group of Applied Materials and then Vice President New Business Ventures Group at ARM - two of the largest global semiconductor companies. 

In 2017 I joined In-Q-Tel (the CIA's VC Partner) as a Senior Partner then MD of the firm's London office. The investments I led for In-Q-Tel include Cerebras (25x current return) Mythic EdgeQ EdgeImpulse (acquired by Qualcomm) Pragmatic and Paragraf.

My successful exits before InQTel include Sequans(IPO) Liquavista (Samsung acquisition) IMS Nano (Intel acquisition) Pinebrook Imaging (Applied Materials acquisition) and Twist Bioscience (IPO). 

At In-Q-Tel I also served as an advisor to government leaders on semiconductor policy starting in 2019 which helped inform the CHIPS act 

In 2022 I decided to start a new endeavor. My former MIT housemate and medical doctor Dr. Tara Bishop and I founded Black Opal Ventures. Our debut $63M fund invests in frontier technology companies improving healthcare and security. I am particularly focused on finding companies that leverage the technology I have seen developed for and by the semiconductor industry combined with advancements in artificial intelligence to advance drug discovery to improve human longevity and to strengthen national security.

We have invested in a number of leading companies including:

- Eridu AI (custom silicon provider for AI networking infrastructure) 
- Authmind (identity security 
- Hyro AI (conversational AI for healthcare systems)
- Outpace (AI enabled drug discovery platform for CAR-T therapeutics )
- Parasail (neocloud for AI inference workloads)
- Ansa (enzyme based gene synthesis platform) 
- Blaze.AI (HIPAA compliant no code platform) 
- Oath Surgical (tech enabled ASCs)
- Flytehealth (tech enabled obesity management )
- Conceivable (AI enabled IVF)



I currently serve on the Board of Notre Dame High School for girls in San Jose. I am passionate about advancing the number of women in the STEM and venture capital fields.

I started out valet parking and working as an intern helping startups go to market before learning to sell at Xerox. In search of a faster growing industry I moved to LinkedIn as we took the business from 100m to 770m members from IPO to acquisition by MSFT and from $600m in revenue to $10B. In those same years as a side project I started a DTC brand with a friend from college helping music lovers fix their earbuds. We grew it to 250 000 customers including partnerships with Best Buy and Shark Tank’s Daymond John. 

In 2016 I began angel investing into companies focused on health wealth and happiness in an attempt to angle my career toward the intersection of my experiences and curiosities. In 2018 I began mentoring at emerging funds and moving closer toward a career in VC. Now at Antler we’re on course to start 10 000 companies over 10 years and by extension will help millions of people experience the magic of entrepreneurship and scaling businesses—two things that have shaped my entire professional life.

company_description:
Healing Realty Trust Inc. (“Healing Realty Trust” “HRT” or the “Company”) is a data-driven self-managed real estate investment company specializing in the ownership of income-generating properties primarily linked to the healthcare industry

The primary goal is to generate income for its shareholders through the acquisition development and management of healthcare real estate assets. These assets are typically leased to healthcare providers or operators on a long-term basis providing a stable source of rental income for investors. 

In addition to providing financial benefits for investors we will also play an important role in the healthcare industry by supporting the development and operation of these facilities. By investing in and owning healthcare related real estate assets we help to ensure that there are adequate facilities available to meet the growing demand for healthcare services.

Healing RT may also work closely with healthcare providers and operators to develop new facilities or upgrade existing ones to meet the needs of patients and communities. This collaboration can result in the creation of state-of-the-art healthcare facilities that provide high-quality care to patients.

The company will become an important player in the healthcare industry providing financial support for the development and operation of these facilities while generating stable returns for its investors.
headline: Founder & SVP of Real Estate at Healing Realty Trust

I am passionate about applying the value investing approach (Buffett/Fisher) to VC startup investments and supporting founders who share my vision of creating innovative and impactful solutions for the global market. company_description:
Blue Gravity Capital is a hands-on investment firm backing businesses with healthy fundamentals across Central Europe with a focus on Poland. We work closely with founders and management teams on professionalization governance commercial execution expansion and strategic initiatives.

We make a small number of investments and commit substantial time to each one. Our model is built on concentration alignment and practical value creation.

We typically look for companies with €5–25M in revenue and positive EBITDA acquiring majority stakes. As active partners we support teams in scaling internationally strengthening governance building world-class organizations and preparing for high-quality exits.

Our team brings together decades of experience across investing M&A finance strategy and company leadership. We have worked with businesses across software manufacturing fintech consumer healthcare and other sectors supporting growth international expansion and institutional development.

What ties that experience together is a practical hands-on approach to building stronger businesses — not simply providing capital.

We're not just investors. We're long-term partners committed to building resilient high-performing businesses.

His main focus is helping Swiss European ans selected US companies gain traction through seed stage investments and Board memberships. He lectures frequently at events throughout Europe and the US and organizes study trips and visits to Boston and Silicon Valley. 

From 2017 to 2024 he was a founding General Partner of Privilege Ventures SA in Lugano a FINMA registered early stage Deep Tech VC. Privilege has a portfolio of about 35 companies many drawing from ETH in Zurich or EPFL in Lausanne.

Before joining EuroUS Ventures in 2005 he launched US operations for itemic AG a German semiconductor software company and was responsible for their worldwide sales and marketing. 

In 1997 he co-founded and became CEO of FabCentric a Yield Management software company where he raised several million dollars in VC funding and which he grew to more than 25 employees before it was sold to another publicly traded software company in late 2001. 

Lucian spent more than 20 years in the semiconductor equipment industry holding a series of positions in Sales and Marketing based out of Switzerland and then Boston MA.

Lucian was born in Germany educated in the French school system and went on to earn a B.Sc. in International Economics from the School of Foreign Service at Georgetown University (with a minor in Russian) and an MBA from INSEAD in 1981.

Bowles is cofounder and Chairman of the Mayor’s Star Council which is created with City of Dallas Mayor Mike Rawlings to engage culturally diverse and civically minded group of young professions to make impact on the city. SCN is national network of professional ecosystem builders working together to support entrepreneurs in their local regions. Bowles was appointed to the National Advisory Council on Innovation and Entrepreneurship (NACIE) as a special government employee by Secretary of Commerce Penny Pritzker. Most recently Trey was appointed to the Center for American Entrepreneurship Board which is a nonpartisan 501(c)(3) research policy and advocacy organization that works with policymakers in Washington and across the country to achieve a policy environment that promotes new business formation survival and growth. Bowles a serial entrepreneur having built numerous organizations in both the for-profit and non-profit space lives in Dallas TX with his bride and three kids.

company_description:
Intravitreal anti-VEGF therapy has revolutionized the management of major retinal diseases with revenues projected to reach $19 billion by 2029. Yet a significant unmet need remains: patients must endure frequent in-office injections and monitoring while real-world outcomes continue to fall short of clinical trial results.

A key driver of this treatment burden is the rate of drug clearance. Current practice addresses this by injecting large amounts of drug well above the clinically relevant dose to extend the treatment window to roughly 3–4 months for a subset of patients.

Re-Vana takes a fundamentally different approach. Instead of delivering high excess doses our technologies enable sustained controlled release of smaller amounts of drug maintaining therapeutic concentrations for 6–12 months without the peaks troughs and treatment gaps that compromise patient outcomes. By reducing injections to twice yearly-or potentially once yearly without surgery we aim to keep patients consistently within the therapeutic window. Our approach has the potential to improve real-world outcomes while dramatically reducing treatment burden.

The treatment burden and drug clearance challenge affects all retinal therapies representing an enormous opportunity for Re-Vana. Our proprietary photo crosslinked biodegradable hydrogel technologies. EyeLief® and OcuLief® are designed to avoid inflammation and to protect biologics from aggregation and degradation. Re-Vana's technologies are therapeutic-modality agnostic (including Biologics Peptide Large and Small molecules) and have been successfully applied to more than 20 different molecules.

Re-Vana is advancing eye health through both internal pipeline programs and strategic collaborations with leading ophthalmology companies. In July 2025 Re-Vana and Boehringer Ingelheim announced a strategic collaboration to develop long-acting ophthalmic therapies.
headline: Partner at ExSight Ventures

current_position_description:
BioProtonics has developed and patented a new technology platform (i.e. methods and software) called Magnetic Resonance Histopathology (MRH) that works on existing Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) devices. 
For the first time MRH provides meaningful MR diagnostic imaging resolution on tissue at the micro-level (sub 1mm). Also very important for broad commercialization MRH adds less than 1 minute to current MRI scan time and corrects for patient movement during scans. MRH potentially will lead to a safer less invasive less costly alternative for many biopsies routinely performed today of which the majority are negative. This advancement will allow for earlier detection of many diseases and cancers and enable more accurate therapies sooner in the disease cycle to improve patient outcomes and lower medical costs. Eventually MRH could be part of every MRI scan.
headline: BioProtonics CEO

about:
With decades of experience in Life Sciences and Digital fields and a scientific background in molecular biology and immunovirology (PhD from the Pasteur Institute France) I first got to grips with bioinformatics tools and code in the early 90s whilst working on viral phylogenetic studies.

After my PhD I joined the effort at Genoscope (Evry France) in ramping up the human genome project putting in place the automatic gene annotation system for human chromosome 14. In 2001 I moved to the European Bioinformatics Institute (Cambridge UK) where I led the Ensembl comparative genomics team until 2007 under Ewan Birney’s direct leadership.

I then founded Eagle Genomics shortly before graduating from the Cambridge Judge Business School MBA program in 2008. For the 12 years of that journey I have been a driving force within the executive team at Eagle Genomics supporting the strategy and partnership activities as well as setting up a world-class company operation able to adapt to market demand. This has enabled the company to sustain its growth and extend its activities horizontally from the traditional pharma/biotech sector into sectors as diverse as plant breeding animal health consumer goods and food safety

Since April 2020 I've been involved in seed investment first as Investment Director at CMS Ventures and now as Venture Partner at Nina Capital bringing my entrepreneurial experience Life Sciences & Digital expertise to unearth the most exciting and innovative startups in the UK ecosystem. I have not completely abandoned the entrepreneurial path and I continue to be involved in mentoring startups and coaching founders in my close network as well as getting involved in some of them more formally in stealth mode at the moment.

about:
I am an entrepreneur turned investor and one of the few women who founded a venture fund in Canada. I am passionate about driving growth and empowering the startup ecosystem. 

I was a key founding team member at Wattpad a revolutionary storytelling platform. During my tenure I helped them scale the company from tens of thousands of users to tens of millions contributing to its incredible success and market dominance. Wattpad's remarkable growth culminated in one of Canada's largest exits in the past decade when Naver in South Korea acquired the company for over US$600 million in 2021.

Inspired by my operating experiences not only at Wattpad but also with my 20 years in tech I turned myself into an investor and co-founded Two Small Fish Ventures an early-stage VC fund. We back early-stage products platforms and protocols that transform user behaviour and empower businesses and individuals to unlock new values. I am deeply committed to identifying and supporting the next generation of groundbreaking startups leveraging my firsthand knowledge of scaling businesses to drive their success.

Beyond my role as an investor I am an active mentor in the tech community. I find great joy in sharing my expertise and experience with aspiring founders and helping them navigate the challenges of building and growing their startups.
company_description:
Two Small Fish Ventures (TSF) is a seed-stage deep tech venture capital firm that invests globally in the next frontier of computing and its applications. For more than a decade TSF has backed multiple breakout successes across consumer enterprise industrial and dual-use markets and has consistently demonstrated its ability to spot the next major opportunities early.

TSF invests in five interconnected areas: Vertical AI Platforms Physical AI (e.g. robotics and smart machines) AI Infrastructure Advanced Computing Hardware (e.g. semiconductors and quantum) and Smart Energy. The firm supports founders at the earliest stages primarily pre-revenue when foundational technology is ready for commercialization. TSF makes five to seven high-conviction early-stage investments per year.

The TSF team represents a rare combination. All partners are engineers with deep operating experience the team includes a former CEO serial entrepreneurs and startup leaders who have built scaled and exited category-defining companies before becoming deep tech investors. This full-cycle expertise gives TSF a unique ability to assess deep tech opportunities and provide unmatched hands-on support from technology through commercialization after investment. This support is strengthened by long-standing relationships with a network of top founders research institutions and technical ecosystems.

about: Minimally Invasive Spinal Surgeon
company_description:
Kleiner Device Labs - Advancing Minimally Invasive Spinal Surgery™

During 25 years of active spinal surgical practice and over 6 000 surgeries Dr. Jeffrey Kleiner became increasingly aware that instrument design and quality played a significant role in the clinical outcome of spinal surgery. He recognized key gaps in the instrumentation available to spinal surgeons and this led him to develop a series of innovative solutions. 

In 2013 Dr. Kleiner established Kleiner Device Labs to build and sell innovative spinal surgical products. These novel and advanced tools deliver improvements in efficiency and safety for patients and enable surgeons to perform more effective and less complicated fusion surgery. 

Kleiner Device Lab's first product a Bi-Directional Graft Funnel (KG™1) works with any flowable biologic graft material provides a more efficient and safer delivery vehicle for bone graft extenders facilitates grafting in front of or alongside all types of interbody cages and can eliminate or reduce surgeon dependency on BMP.

Building on the underlying philosophy behind the KG™1 Kleiner Device Labs developed the KG™2 Surge™ Flow-Thru Interbody Implant in 2021. No other device integrates implant positioning and graft delivery like the KG™2 Surge™. The system not only maximizes successful outcomes for patients (while simplifying the procedure for surgeons) but reduces costs for healthcare providers and insurers as well.

Kleiner Device Labs has a broad patent portfolio and a runway of innovative minimally invasive spinal surgical products. These strategies are equally effective in open theater surgical technique as well.
headline:
* President & CEO Kleiner Device Labs
* Surgeon at Spine Consultants PC

company_description:
Chronic conditions like cancer remain difficult to diagnose accurately due to the limitations of current imaging tools. Cancer is the most misdiagnosed of the 'big three' conditions. While MRI and PET-CT often miss small findings ultrasound is highly operator-dependent and inconsistent leaving a critical gap in the early and reliable detection of diseases.
Revealan™ our robotic ultrasound imaging platform seamlessly integrates with any off-the-shelf ultrasound device to enable operator-agnostic automated AI-enabled high-resolution 3D imaging. The robotic scan output is a series of standardized medical images with a density at least 10 times higher than that of an MRI. Our solution represents a transformative leap forward not only in the diagnosis and monitoring of various conditions but also in surgical planning. It stands alone as the premier solution offering comprehensive mapping to facilitate personalized treatment. Revealan™ is optimizing ultrasound for better patient outcomes.
headline: Translating science into strategy. Turning strategy into value.

about: Early stage venture capitalist investing in seed and pre-seed European tech companies. Proud and humbled to be nicknamed “the least dumdum investor” by my favorite founder.
company_description:
We take risks on brilliant people and products. We work with founders on a mission to change the one thing they think is fundamentally broken in the world. We welcome young or first-time founders who are technical or domain experts in their field.

Our focus is finding Europe's best early stage tech startups and building them into large revenue category-defining companies. We believe great companies are built by great teams not by venture capitalists. Some might call our approach old-school.

We lead pre-seed or seed rounds. We invest at fair terms and reserve capital to continue investing through a company's journey. We typically invest between $500k to $5 million although we have gone as low as $250 000 and as high as $10 million. We like to aim for an ownership position between 10% to 20%.

When we believe in your vision market and team we get conviction quickly. We often write the first large check a company receives. We aren't afraid of being the only investor but are happy to invest alongside others.

We work hard behind the scenes to maximize value. We provide introductions help make key hires provide market intel ink strategic partnerships and handle difficult operational issues. Sometimes we might even spark crazy acquisition offers. Often our work is providing a sage perspective that comes from living through multiple market cycles and having the forthrightness to hold honest conversations others shy away from.

Having spent most of our working lives in Silicon Valley we aim to replicate in Europe what we saw work in California. We dream big and are unashamedly ambitious for our companies.

about:
Investment Professional and Entrepreneur Driving Innovation and Growth

Eduardo Kupper is a seasoned investment professional and entrepreneur with over a decade of experience in venture capital private equity and tech startups. As the Founding Partner at Airborne Ventures he focuses on investing in B2B companies that drive efficiency and productivity in financial services and healthcare leveraging technology to transform these sectors.

Previously at Inovabra Ventures (Bradesco's VC arm) he oversaw R$700 million in invested capital playing a pivotal role in shaping Brazil’s CVC ecosystem. With R$800 million managed across two previous funds and 80+ deals done his track record includes two +$200 million exits through a Corporate sale and an IPO and a De-SPAC. His expertise spans Venture Capital Entrepreneurship and Tech Startups with a focus on SaaS Cloud AI/ML Real Estate and Health. Recognized as a Top GP by VClab and Decile Partner he has co-invested with major VCs across Brazil and the US.

Eduardo holds an MBA from Wharton an MA from The Lauder Institute and a B.Eng. in Electrical Engineering from Unicamp blending technical financial and strategic expertise to drive innovation. Passionate about empowering founders and fostering growth he continues to shape the future of venture capital and tech ecosystems.

Connect to learn more about his work investments or insights!
company_description:
Most early-stage funds in LATAM invest in trends. We invest in obsession.
Airborne Ventures is an early-stage fund backing execution-driven founders in Healthtech B2B SaaS and Fintech across Brazil and LATAM — at the stages where most institutional capital won't go.
We've seen this market for nearly two decades. Through IPOs M&As banking VC arms and 70+ investments as angels and GPs. We know what separates a market leader from a cautionary tale — and it's never the pitch deck.
Our edge is simple: discipline over hype and narratives and exits over optics.
The results:
$200M+ in exits · 9.5x+ MOIC · Portfolio companies that grew from R$30M → R$250M in revenue
No FOMO. No imported frameworks. Just capital with conviction — and the scar tissue to back it up.
For LPs: We offer concentrated exposure to LATAM's most resilient early-stage companies built by founders obsessed with real problems in markets that are too large to ignore and too complex for generalist funds to navigate.
For founders: If you're building something that matters and need a partner who's been in the trenches — let's talk.

about:
Nmachi Jidenma currently works on the investment team at General Catalyst where she partners with exceptional founders to help build enduring companies. Prior to joining General Catalyst Nmachi was a Senior Director at Sequoia Capital. Prior to Sequoia Capital Nmachi was a global strategy and business development lead at WhatsApp Inc the world’s leader in mobile messaging with over 3 billion monthly users. 

In this role she led WhatsApp’s strategy and business development efforts to drive monetization and build an ecosystem of business messaging partners worldwide building AI/ML messaging customer service and commerce tools on the WhatsApp Business platform from the ground up. In addition she led strategic alliances with WhatsApp's first set of business partners developers and some of its biggest and most important customers such as Netflix Uber Airbnb and Twilio as well as partnerships with governments nonprofits and United Nations agencies such as the World Health Organization and UNICEF to drive WhatsApp’s COVID-19 vaccine response and its long term usage for social good. These efforts positively impacted the lives of tens of millions of people. She also led global teams on the rollout of WhatsApp Payments as well as early monetization strategy initiatives spanning business model pricing infrastructure alliances and landscape assessments to help inform its M&A strategy.

Prior to WhatsApp she led partnerships at Facebook with financial services industry leaders such as PayPal Visa American Express and MasterCard and drove adoption of Facebook payments products. Prior to Facebook Nmachi drove business development at PayPal for its billion dollar mobile disruptors portfolio where she launched the company into verticals such as mobile marketplaces and cryptocurrencies through partnerships with innovative startups & companies like Coinbase. Earlier in her career Nmachi worked in a variety of roles spanning strategy business development and investment banking.

Nmachi serves as a Member of the Board of Trustees of the Goddy Jidenma Foundation and has served as a board director/mentor/advisor to early-stage funds and startups and as an angel investor. She is a frequent speaker at various conferences and panels with appearances at Fortune Conferences Techonomy Harvard Business School Stanford Graduate School of Business Money2020 VivaTech etc. Her work has been featured / recognized in a wide variety of publications such as CNN Reuters and Business Insider. She holds a Master’s in Applied Economics from Cornell University.

company_description: Gaingels is a leading venture investment syndicate in support of and representing the LGBTQIA+ community and allies in the venture capital space. Gaingels also supports its companies with a diverse talent and capital pipeline and provides an internal diverse board member recruitment advisory to help bring more diversity and representation within private boards. Gaingels invests in companies with diverse/LGBTQIA+ founders and C-suite leaders at all stages of growth as well as in other high-growth companies resolved on building more inclusive teams. Gaingels is an ardent supporter the Diversity Term Sheet Rider Initiative to increase access to venture funding events for non-traditional check writers and regularly co-invests with select VC leads across a variety of sectors from technology to B2B healthcare and consumer in competitive and over-subscribed rounds from Seed to Growth/pre-IPO.
